notes.note: Cicero and Atticus had been debating whether the Latin verb inhibere ("to hold back, check") could render the Greek Stoic-Academic term epoche, the suspension of assent. Cicero now rejects it, having learned the word is a rowing command that actually denotes vigorous reverse motion, not a halt.
Epoche is the Skeptics' "suspension of judgement" --- withholding assent from any proposition. The technical term is central to the Academica Cicero is composing.

Carneades of Cyrene (214-129 BC), the great head of the skeptical New Academy, who likened the suspension of assent to a boxer dropping his guard or a charioteer reining in --- a checking, not a reversal.

Probably C. Asinius Pollio, then with Caesar's forces in Spain; Cicero affects to care more about a fine point of philosophical vocabulary than about current news. Identification flagged as a judgment bind.

Cicero asks for news of a Metellus, but which of the several Caecilii Metelli is meant cannot be securely fixed; no registered Metellus is a clean contemporary fit. Flagged for the registry; left untagged.
